original_hypothesis.title: FixedTermLoanHooks fixedTermEndTime truncation to uint32 may create term validation/storage mismatch after year-2106 rollover
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FixedTermLoanHooks reads fixedTermEndTime via calldataload into a uint32 variable in assembly, but validates using 256-bit arithmetic against block.timestamp. Today block.timestamp fits in uint32, but after the 2106 timestamp overflow boundary, truncation could cause stored fixedTermEndTime to differ from the validated value, potentially allowing immediate withdrawals or incorrectly enforcing lockups.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Low immediacy but a demonstrable code defect in time encoding that can cause dead-end or bypass states in long-lived deployments. Phase D should decide whether protocol lifetime assumptions make this relevant and whether defensive checks should enforce uint32 bounds explicitly.

proof_of_signal.pattern: assembly `fixedTermEndTime := calldataload(hooksData.offset)` into uint32; later comparisons use block.timestamp (uint256) and then stored in HookedMarket.fixedTermEndTime (uint32)

preconditions: Markets still use FixedTermLoanHooks and rely on fixedTermEndTime correctness

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Mixed-width time handling (uint32 storage / uint256 validation) can diverge on overflow-era timestamps.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Post-2106 or in simulated chains with high timestamps

operational_questions: Is there any explicit protocol deprecation/migration plan before 2106?
Do any chains in scope use non-standard timestamp semantics that could accelerate this issue (e.g., testing/forks)?
validation_requirements: Simulate with a fork/test chain where timestamp > uint32.max and confirm behavior divergence
Add explicit bound checks that the input fixedTermEndTime fits uint32 and matches validated value

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Operate on a chain/time where timestamp overflow is relevant
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Create market with hooksData fixedTermEndTime that validates in 256-bit space but truncates in storage
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Exploit mismatch to bypass withdraw lock or lock funds unexpectedly
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Cause operational failure in fixed-term guarantees

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium (in affected era): fixed-term product semantics break; lenders/borrowers cannot rely on lockups
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High for immutable deployments if still in use

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected immediate withdrawals or perpetual lockups in time-overflow simulations
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Mismatch between events emitted (FixedTermUpdated) and stored term behavior

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
